column2: The Strait of Hormuz remains closed on Day 24. Brent crude trades near $112 and WTI near $98, as markets weigh US-Iranian statements — oil is little moved after Iran offered partial passage to 'neutral' vessels while the US has not yet responded. Gold fell sharply from its March highs to near $4,373/oz, posting its worst weekly decline in decades as Fed hawkishness and dollar strength weighed. The VN-Index dropped 2.08% to 1,614 on Monday in a broad Asian equity sell-off. The SBV reference rate rose 5 VND to 25,090 VND/USD; the Vietcombank sell rate hit 26,344 — touching the +5% band ceiling. The chợ đen (free market) rate stands at 27,950-28,000 VND/USD, a 6.4% premium above the band ceiling. US retail gasoline averaged $3.942/gal (AAA), up from $2.97 pre-crisis. Vietnam's MOIT fuel price unchanged; next adjustment date TBD. TTF gas opened at 61.5 EUR/MWh Monday as European gas markets extend their surge on LNG supply tightness from Qatar damage.
US launches airstrikes on Iranian nuclear facilities; Iran threatens Strait closure
IRGC Navy deploys mines and fast boats across Hormuz shipping lanes
Ship transits drop to zero; Brent surges past $86/bbl
All major carriers announce Hormuz transit suspension
Vietnam MOIT emergency fuel price adjustment; RON95 to 22340 VND/L. P&I clubs withdraw war-risk coverage.
Vietnam Govt issues Resolution 36 on petroleum price stabilization
EIA confirms Brent at $94/bbl; Israel strikes Iran South Pars gas field
PM Pham Minh Chinh directs fuel price stabilization; Price Stabilization Fund activated. RON95 briefly hits 29120.
Extraordinary adjustment: RON95 pulled back to 25240 (heavy PSF subsidy)
SBV intervenes; VN-Index falls below 1700. Free market USD/VND hits 27096
RON95 surges 20% to 30690 VND/L; diesel +24% to 33420; PM calls partners for oil supply
Brent hits $113.40. Vietnam secures 4M barrels from partners. VN-Index drops to 1648.
Day 22 of crisis. Goldman warns oil may stay triple-digits for years.
Free market USD/VND hits 27307. SBV reference rate unchanged at 25085. Iran says it will target US/Israeli assets if energy facilities struck.

VN-Index falls 2.08% to 1614. SBV ref rate +5 VND to 25090. Vietcombank sell hits band ceiling 26344. Chợ đen USD/VND surges to 27950-28000. Gold near $4373 (-10% weekly). Oil steady as markets weigh Iran-US rhetoric.
